text: Editing human DNA could change life as we know it—but should it? The world watched in shock after He Jiankui edited embryos, sparking a fierce debate on ethics. Most scientists now agree: the risks of germline editing are immense and unpredictable. Potential genetic mutations or unforeseen health issues could arise, affecting generations. Ethical lines are being drawn; many fear crossing them could lead to a dystopian future. What do you think? Should we edit the human genome? Comment below!
